mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in housing starts above 1500 units signals a strengthening demand for housing, which boosts consumer confidence and spending, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 1-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in the unemployment rate below 4.5% signals a tightening labor market, which boosts wage growth and consumer spending, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in late-cycle expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in the federal funds rate above 5.5% signals a tightening monetary cycle that increases discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in tightening periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in real personal consumption expenditures above 0.5% signals a strengthening demand for goods and services, which boosts economic growth and leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 1-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in industrial production above 0.5% signals a strengthening supply chain, which boosts economic growth and leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 1-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in the federal funds rate and a 2-month increase in the consumer price index above 0.5% signal a monetary policy mistake that increases inflation expectations and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in policy mistake periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in payroll employment above 200,000 and a 3-month decline in the unemployment rate below 4.5% signal a tightening labor market that boosts wage growth and consumer spending, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in late-cycle expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
